images.alt:
A dramatic documentary-style movie poster dominated by the close-up face of a young Black woman staring directly at the viewer through smoke, dust, and firelight. Her expression is exhausted yet unbroken, with dirt and ash covering her skin and tangled fabric wrapped tightly around the lower half of her face like a survival scarf. The lighting is warm and apocalyptic, bathing the entire composition in deep oranges, glowing ambers, and smoky black shadows. Across the center of the image, the title “Spirit of Survival” is painted in large rough white brushstroke lettering, partially blending into flames and drifting smoke.
Along the left side of the poster, bold stacked text reads: “Survival is not just instinct. It’s spirit.” Smaller documentary branding appears at the top with the words “A Documentary Film.” Beneath the title, silhouettes of displaced people walk through a burning forest illuminated by a blinding sunset or wildfire glow. Two additional figures stand in the lower corners, one wrapped heavily against harsh conditions and another partially obscured in darkness, reinforcing themes of endurance, migration, and resilience. The bottom section contains festival laurels, production credits, and a “Coming Soon” banner styled like a prestige documentary release. The entire image carries a gritty cinematic realism with layered smoke textures, glowing embers, and intense emotional focus.
Keywords:
documentary, survival, wildfire, resilience, cinematic, refugees, smoke, firelight, portrait, dystopian, endurance, humanity
AI-generated; use is permitted for non-commercial creative projects. MidJourney, FLUX.1-schnell model via Perchance, Artspace.ai, REVE, Echoform™.

images.alt: First-person perspective from floor level inside a vast ceremonial fox-temple. A fox-eared priestess stands directly over the viewer, looking down with one eyebrow subtly raised and a calm, knowing expression that suggests equal parts patience, amusement, and inevitability. Her hands rest firmly on her hips, posture strong and balanced, conveying authority without hostility. She wears flowing dark crimson and gold ritual garments adorned with delicate chains and filigree accents. Long black hair falls around her shoulders, catching shafts of warm golden light descending from above. Her stance is grounded and confident, one leg slightly forward, creating the impression that the viewer has just ended up exactly where she expected them to. The camera angle emphasizes her height and presence, while the background remains softly blurred: towering banners, warm torchlight, hints of temple architecture, and faint fox motifs. The focus remains entirely on the priestess herself—her gaze, posture, and expression communicating a silent, almost compassionate: "Well... what did you think was going to happen?"

Here's the deal with research prompts

Tell your AI exactly what you're looking into, why it matters, and how you want the results. It's basically a research brief. Clear specs mean better results.

What actually works:

Figure out your scope before you touch the keyboard. Decide if you want an overview or a deep dive. And mention your timeline — the AI doesn't know if you need this for tomorrow or next month.

The setting is the deck of a decrepit, cursed schooner called the Antelope, tossing on a violent, storm-tossed Atlantic ocean under a bruised, purple-black sky. I’m dressed in tattered 1778 sailor garb—a rough wool coat, a loose linen shirt, and a red bandana tied around my head. My face is smudged with soot and sea spray, and my expression is a mix of terror and grim determination as I stare down a massive rogue wave. The rigging is tangled and snapping, and the sails are tattered shreds. The texture is gritty, with deep cross-hatching and heavy shadows to emphasize the chaos of the sea. The vibe is historical, desperate, and hauntingly epic.
